goal: Implement the Log MCP Service (TOKEN_USE.md §3.1): a new Go REST server (log-server) that stores and queries structured session logs, exposed to the agent via a stdio MCP wrapper, following all existing security and infrastructure patterns.

tasks.name: Log-server Go REST service
tasks.action: Create a new Go REST server at cluster/log-server/ modeled on tester-server and mcp-server patterns. Implements: (1) HTTPS on :8443 with internal CA certs, (2) Bearer token auth via LOG_API_TOKEN using crypto/subtle.ConstantTimeCompare, (3) /health endpoint, (4) SQLite storage for structured log events (LLM calls, tool calls, file reads, test runs, git ops, plan ops — schema per TOKEN_USE.md §3.1 'Log Events to Capture' table), (5) POST /ingest endpoint accepting JSON log events with session_id, (6) GET /sessions (list_sessions: limit, since params → session IDs + summary stats), (7) GET /sessions/{id}/summary (get_session_summary: total LLM calls, tokens, tool counts, duration), (8) POST /sessions/{id}/query (query_logs: event_type filter, time_range → matching entries), (9) GET /sessions/{id}/tokens (get_token_breakdown: per-call input/output/cache tokens, model). Add entrypoint.sh following existing pattern: env var scan for forbidden tokens (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, MCP_API_TOKEN, PLAN_API_TOKEN, TESTER_API_TOKEN, GIT_API_TOKEN, CLAUDE_API_TOKEN, DYNAMIC_AGENT_KEY) then exec the binary. Storage in /logs bind mount, rotated by session. cap_drop ALL, mem_limit 256m, cpus 0.5, pids_limit 50.
tasks.verify: go build ./... succeeds; go vet ./... clean; entrypoint.sh is executable and rejects forbidden env vars in unit test
tasks.done: main.go compiles, implements all 4 query endpoints + ingest + health, entrypoint.sh scans env vars, go.mod declares module

tasks.name: Log MCP stdio wrapper
tasks.action: Create log_mcp.py following the exact pattern of tester_mcp.py / plan_mcp.py. Import from mcp.server + mcp.server.stdio. Read LOG_SERVER_URL and LOG_API_TOKEN from runenv. Define 4 MCP tools matching TOKEN_USE.md spec: (1) list_sessions(limit, since) → GET /sessions, (2) get_session_summary(session_id) → GET /sessions/{id}/summary, (3) query_logs(session_id, event_type, time_range) → POST /sessions/{id}/query, (4) get_token_breakdown(session_id) → GET /sessions/{id}/tokens. All calls use requests with headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {LOG_API_TOKEN}'}, verify='/app/certs/ca.crt', timeout=30. Use _dispatch pattern with error handling returning CallToolResult(content=[TextContent], isError=bool). Keep tool descriptions concise to minimize context bloat (per TOKEN_USE.md design principles).
tasks.verify: python -c 'import log_mcp' succeeds; tool count == 4; run_tests passes
tasks.done: log_mcp.py defines 4 MCP tools, uses stdio_server entrypoint, reads LOG_SERVER_URL/LOG_API_TOKEN from runenv

tasks.name: Claude-server wiring
tasks.action: Wire the log-server into claude-server: (1) runenv.py — add LOG_SERVER_URL = os.getenv('LOG_SERVER_URL', 'https://log-server:8443') and LOG_API_TOKEN = os.getenv('LOG_API_TOKEN'), following existing pattern. (2) .mcp.json — add 'logs' entry: {command: 'mcp-watchdog', args: ['--verbose', '--', 'python3', '/app/log_mcp.py']}. (3) verify_isolation.py — add 'log-server' role with forbidden env vars (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, DYNAMIC_AGENT_KEY, MCP_API_TOKEN, PLAN_API_TOKEN, TESTER_API_TOKEN, GIT_API_TOKEN, CLAUDE_API_TOKEN) and required (LOG_API_TOKEN); update 'claude-server' role to require LOG_API_TOKEN and add LOG_API_TOKEN to its forbidden list for other roles (mcp-server, plan-server, tester-server, git-server). Update token isolation matrix comments.
tasks.verify: run_tests passes; verify_isolation.py role 'log-server' rejects ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and requires LOG_API_TOKEN; .mcp.json has 6 server entries
tasks.done: runenv.py exports LOG_SERVER_URL + LOG_API_TOKEN; .mcp.json includes 'logs' server; verify_isolation.py has log-server role and updated token matrix

tasks.name: Docker infrastructure
tasks.action: Add log-server container to the cluster: (1) Dockerfile.log — multi-stage Go build (golang:1.24-alpine builder → alpine:3.21 runtime), copy binary + entrypoint.sh + certs (ca.crt, log.crt, log.key), create appuser UID 1000, EXPOSE 8443, ENTRYPOINT ['/app/entrypoint.sh']. Follow Dockerfile.tester pattern. (2) docker-compose.yml — add log-server service: build context cluster/log-server with Dockerfile.log, int_net only, cap_drop ALL, mem_limit 256m, cpus 0.5, pids_limit 50, volumes [./logs:/logs:rw], environment [LOG_API_TOKEN from .secrets.env], depends_on none. Add LOG_API_TOKEN to claude-server environment. Add log-server leaf cert generation to run.sh cert generation section (openssl req + sign with internal CA, SAN=log-server). Add LOG_API_TOKEN generation to run.sh token generation section (openssl rand -hex 32). Mount logs/ directory.
tasks.verify: docker compose config validates; Dockerfile.log builds; run.sh generates LOG_API_TOKEN and Dockerfile.log generates log-server cert like has been done on other dockerfiles
tasks.done: Dockerfile.log exists and builds; docker-compose.yml defines log-server with correct security constraints; run.sh generates token; Dockerfile.log generates log cert

tasks.name: Structured log emission in server.py
tasks.action: Add structured JSON log emission to server.py so log-server has data to query. (1) After each claude subprocess completes, parse the --output-format json response to extract: model, input_tokens, output_tokens, cache_creation_input_tokens, cache_read_input_tokens, duration_ms, tool calls (name, request/response sizes). (2) POST each event to LOG_SERVER_URL/ingest with session_id (generated per /ask or /plan request), event_type, timestamp, and fields per the schema. (3) Use a background thread or fire-and-forget async POST so log emission never blocks the response. (4) Apply _redact_secrets() to any logged content. (5) If LOG_SERVER_URL is not set or POST fails, log a warning and continue (log emission is non-critical). Import LOG_SERVER_URL, LOG_API_TOKEN from runenv.
tasks.verify: run_tests passes; server.py emits structured log events after subprocess completion; failure to reach log-server does not break /ask or /plan
tasks.done: server.py extracts token counts from claude output and POSTs structured events to log-server; emission is fire-and-forget with graceful degradation

tasks.name: Unit tests for log-server and log_mcp
tasks.action: Write unit tests: (1) main_test.go — test auth rejection (missing/wrong token → 401), test /health returns 200, test /ingest + /sessions round-trip (ingest events then query back), test /sessions/{id}/summary aggregation, test /sessions/{id}/tokens breakdown, test entrypoint.sh rejects forbidden env vars. Use httptest.NewServer. (2) test_log_mcp.py — test list_tools returns 4 tools with correct names, test _dispatch routes to correct endpoints, mock requests.post/get to verify URL construction and auth headers. Follow existing test patterns in the repo.
tasks.verify: go test ./... passes in cluster/log-server/; pytest test_log_mcp.py passes; run_tests passes
tasks.done: main_test.go covers auth, health, ingest+query round-trip, summary, token breakdown; test_log_mcp.py covers tool listing and dispatch routing
